Asanas.Title: Stage III
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In/RN] -> [Deep Out/LN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, with external retention with Uddiyana bandha]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for as long as you can to your capacity without strain. Increase the duration of the external retention gradually by one or two seconds as the practice becomes stable. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage X
Asanas.Shorthand: [sitting] [Both In - Normal] ↔ [Both Out - Normal] -> Hold - ALAP -> [Includes Bahya Kumbhaka]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Inhale normally through the nose. Exhale steadily and slowly to empty the lungs as far as possible without straining. Hold the breath while the lungs are empty. Remain completely passive during this external retention. Monitor the abdomen for constriction and the temples for any sensation of pressure. Reduce the length of the retention if you feel a need to gasp for air. Transition into the next inhalation with a smooth and controlled movement. Repeat these cycles for ten to twelve repetitions or continue the practice for ten minutes. Alternate these cycles with normal breathing if any fatigue occurs in the body or mind. Take a few deep breaths to conclude the session. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind completely.

Asanas.Title: Stage III
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep Flat Tongue Hissing] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [ANPC Out - Deep] -> [Slow inhalation with flat tongue, making hissing sound then exhale through alternating partially closed nostrils, Jallandhara bandha only for exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the chin. Keep the head level and looking forward. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Open the mouth and keep the lips slightly apart. Protrude the tip of the tongue only slightly between the lips while keeping the tongue flat. Draw in air over the surface of the flat tongue with a hissing sound. Fill the lungs completely with the cool air. Withdraw the tongue and close the mouth. Lower the head toward the trunk. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for five to ten seconds. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the tip of the thumb. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and completely through the narrowed right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee once the lungs are empty. Raise the head to a level position. Open the mouth and protrude the flat tongue slightly again. Inhale deeply over the flat tongue to fill the lungs to the brim. Withdraw the tongue and close the mouth. Lower the head to reform the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Perform the root lock (Mula Bandha) and retain the breath for five to ten seconds. Raise the right hand back to the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and completely through the narrowed left nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles of alternating exhalations for five to ten minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling normally through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] -> Hold (10s) -> [Both Out - Deep] -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils, Antara Kumbhaka then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through both nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Partially open both nostrils with the tips of the thumb and the fingers. Make the inner walls of the nasal passages parallel to and equidistant from the septum. Exhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the narrowed nostrils without using any force. Adjust the finger tips evenly to monitor and equalise the volume of out-flow on each side. Empty the lungs completely. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Remain passive in this empty state without inhalation for five seconds to perform pensive external retention. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage Ib
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [Alt Out - Deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (8s) -> Deep inhalation through open nostrils, Antara Kumbhaka and Mula Bandha for 10-15 seconds, then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating then Bahya Kumbhaka with Uddiyana Bandha
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Hold the breath for one or two seconds to bring the right hand to the nostrils. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the nasal septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the help of the thumb tip. Ensure that no breath escapes through the left nostril. Lower the right hand and rest it on the right knee. Inhale deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Hold the breath for one or two seconds while raising the right hand to the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the finger tips and ensure no breath escapes through the right nostril. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage XII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [deep/RN] -> [deep/LN] -> Hold Out + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Long inhalation, interrupted exhalation, external retention]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the right nostril firmly by pressing the thumb against the nose. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage XII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Normal] -> [Both Out - Strong Deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Bahya Kumbhaka with Uddiyana Bandha]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Inhale normally through the nose. Exhale steadily and strongly to empty the lungs as far as possible without force, jerk, or harshness. Pause the breath immediately once the exhalation is complete. Refrain from any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the abdominal muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this internal grip for as long as you can without straining. Relax the abdominal muscles first when tension is felt. Bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Inhale slowly and quietly through the nose. Repeat eight to ten such cycles of external retention. Practice three or four deep breaths between each retention cycle to avoid gasping. Increase the duration of the retention gradually as your capacity improves. Maintain the chin lock and the stability of the spine throughout the entire practice. Finish the session with a final inhalation. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind completely.

Asanas.Title: Stage III
Asanas.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Normal] -> [Deep breathing, training to lengthen the inhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line without caving in the rib cage. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls and direct the gaze inwards. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Relax the diaphragm and stretch it sideways. Take a slow and deep in-breath through the nose. Maintain a steady and sibilant sound during the inhalation. Ensure that both lungs fill evenly from the bottom to the top. Keep the abdomen from inflating by preventing the diaphragm from moving above the floating ribs. Listen to the sound of the breath attentively to maintain a constant rhythm. Fill the lungs completely until the sound of the inhalation becomes inaudible. Draw the eye-balls consciously downward to gaze into the lungs. Immobilize the diaphragm at the very start of the exhalation. Breathe out slowly and allow the out-breath to be slightly longer than a normal breath. Continue this pattern of deep inhalation and controlled exhalation for ten minutes. Release the blankets and lie flat in savasana to relax the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VI
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] -> Hold (15s) -> [Both Out - Strong Deep]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale through the nose without strain until the lungs feel empty. Inhale for two or three seconds through the nose. Lightly grip the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again for another two or three seconds without letting the diaphragm loose. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation until the lungs are completely full. Repeat the pauses four or five times until full capacity is reached. Hold the breath at the top of the inhalation for two or three seconds. Grip the abdominal organs and move them toward the spine. Exhale through the nose for two seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again for two seconds. Grip the diaphragm and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation until the lungs feel completely emptied. Repeat the pauses four or five times until the air is fully expelled. Release the grip on the diaphragm and the abdomen gradually during the out-flow of breath. Complete one cycle of interrupted inhalation and interrupted exhalation. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes or for as long as no strain is felt. Take two or three normal breaths through the nose. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IX
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Strong Deep] -> [Both Out - Pauses] -> [Exhalation with pauses sitting]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale until the lungs feel empty without straining. Inhale for two or three seconds through the nose. Lightly grip the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again for another two or three seconds without letting the diaphragm loose. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation until the lungs are completely full. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds while maintaining the internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha). Exhale through the nose for two seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again for two seconds. Grip the diaphragm and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation until the lungs feel completely emptied. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for five or six seconds during this external retention. Relax the abdominal grip and the diaphragm. Inhale normally through the nose to complete the cycle. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes or for as long as you do not feel fatigue. Take two or three normal breaths through the nose. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage V
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Long] -> [LN Out - Interrupted] -> [Long inhalation, interrupted exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Modified II (On a chair)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Place a chair over the head before you go up into Sarvangasana. From there, take the legs down on to the chair to support the thighs.
Take the arms over the head and relax. Do not let the shoulders slip off the blankets.
Stay for 3 to 5 minutes. Come down with bent knees and ease yourself off the stool or chair, at the same time pushing it away with the hands. Slide down.
If you have a long trunk you can increase the height by placing a bolster or blankets on the stool. 
If your back is very stiff, keep the stool further away and support the shins. 
If the neck hurts, increase the height of the blankets.

Asanas.Title: Modified II (Standing)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Place a high stool or a small table near a wall or a ledge. Stand in Tadasana, with right side of body by the wall. Raise the right foot on to the stool and press the foot down. Keep the right thigh touching the wall. Stretch the trunk up and turn towards the wall, holding on with the hands wherever possible to give you leverage. Keep the left leg and trunk vertical, and the foot facing forward. Turn as far as you can.
Stay for 20 to 30 seconds, come down then repeat on the other side.

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In / LN] -> [Hold + Mula (20s)] -> [Deep Out / RN] -> [Internal Retention, Mula Bandha]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale deeply through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath after the inhalation is complete. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for fifteen to twenty seconds without allowing any air to escape. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Increase the duration of the retention by five seconds as the capacity improves. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIIIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Pauses/ON] ↔ [Out - Deep/ANPC] -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils with pauses then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale deeply through both nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through both nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face and bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds or for as long as you can without strain. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Release the root lock gradually. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Release the abdominal grip gradually during the exhalation. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this interrupted inhalation until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils and block both sides firmly. Perform the internal retention with the root lock (Mula Bandha) for the same duration as before. Release the root lock. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril. Release the abdominal grip gradually as the air leaves the lungs. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee once the lungs are empty. Perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) again by drawing the abdomen toward the spine and upward. Hold this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and return the abdomen to its normal state. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IVa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep/Partial Close] -> No Hold -> [Both Out - Deep/Partial Close] -> [Hold + Uddiyana (15s)] -> [Both In - Partial Close] → [Both Out - Partial Close] → Hold + Uddiyana → Repeat
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Narrow both nasal passages as much as possible with the tips of the thumb and fingers. Keep the walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Inhale slowly and deeply through the narrowly controlled nostrils without force. Adjust the finger tips evenly to equalise the volume of the smooth in-flow of air. Block both nostrils completely with the centres of the tips of the thumb and the fingers once the lungs are full. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the right knee. Exhale slowly and smoothly through both open nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds during this external retention. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Repeat these cycles according to your ability. Finish the final cycle by inhaling through open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Near a wall)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Sit sideways to a wall and move the buttocks as close as possible to it. Take the legs up one at a time and swivel the body round. Lie down and rest the legs vertically against the wall. Take the arms over the head and relax.
Keep the hips down, straighten the legs and stretch them up. 

Stretch the arms over the head.
Stay for 20 to 30 seconds or even longer.

To come down, bend the knees and turn to the side.

Asanas.Title: Stage XIV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Deep] -> [LN Out - Deep] -> Hold Out (ALAP) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, internal retention, long exhalation, external retention.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep Flat Tongue Hissing] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [Out - Deep BNPC] -> [Slow inhalation with flat tongue, making hissing sound then exhale through both partially closed nostrils, Jallandhara bandha only for exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the chin. Keep the head level and looking forward. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Open the mouth and keep the lips slightly apart. Protrude the tip of the tongue only slightly between the lips while keeping the tongue flat. Draw in air over the surface of the flat tongue with a hissing sound. Fill the lungs completely. Withdraw the tongue and close the mouth. Lower the head toward the trunk. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for five to ten seconds. Raise the right hand toward the nose. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Partially close both nostrils evenly with the tips of the thumb and fingers. Keep the walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and completely through the narrowed nostrils. Regulate the flow of the out-breath evenly on both sides by adjusting the finger pressure delicately. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee once the lungs feel empty. Repeat these cycles for five to ten minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling normally through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In: Pauses/ON] -> [Out: Deep/BNPC] -> Hold Out (5s) -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation then Bahya Kumbhaka without Uddiyana]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana,

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Strong Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Pauses] -> [Exhalation with pauses lying down]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line without caving in the rib cage. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls and direct the gaze inwards. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Place a soft cloth over the eyes to relax the facial muscles. Stay quiet and still for one or two minutes. Exhale quietly through the nose until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Inhale one long deep breath through the nose to fill the lungs completely. Avoid over-straining the chest or lungs during this inhalation. Exhale through the nose for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for two or three seconds. Exhale again for another two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation until the lungs feel completely emptied. Repeat the pauses four or five times until the air is fully expelled. Release the grip on the abdomen gradually throughout the out-breath. Complete one cycle of deep inhalation and interrupted exhalation. Repeat these cycles for seven to ten minutes or for as long as fatigue is not felt. Take a final inhalation to finish the sequence. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep/ANPC] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Out - Normal/ON] -> [Inhale through alternating nostrils and Antara Kumbhaka with blocked nostrils and Mula Bandha then exhale deeply through both open nostrils]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without disturbing the septum. Narrow the right nostril by bringing its outer portion closer toward the septum. Exhale through the narrowed right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Inhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily without disturbing the width of the passage. Synchronize the fine flow of the in-breath with the expansion of the chest. Block both nostrils completely by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose once the lungs are full. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Block the right nostril with the thumb and partially open the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Make the left passage as narrow as possible while keeping the inner wall parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly and steadily through the left nostril. Synchronize the out-flow with the gradual release of the rib-cage extension. Hold the breath for one second when the lungs are empty to adjust the fingers. Keep the right nostril blocked and narrow the left nostril passage. Inhale through the left nostril slowly and steadily until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils and hold the breath for twenty seconds with the root lock (Mula Bandha). Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Block the left nostril with the ring and little fingers and partially open the right nostril with the thumb. Exhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily. Ensure no breath escapes through the left nostril during this process. Hold the breath for a few seconds when the lungs feel completely empty. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the final cycle with an inhalation through the right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Through a door frame)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Support the vertical leg against a door frame or column, with the vertical leg supported from the buttock-bone and follow normal sequence as below.
Lie down on your back. Extend both legs straight out. Inhale and bend your right knee toward your chest. Grip your right big toe with your right hand. Place your left hand on your left thigh to anchor it. Straighten your right leg toward the ceiling. Keep your left leg pressed firmly into the floor. On an exhalation, pull your chin toward your right knee. Inhale and return your head to the floor. Open your right leg out to the side toward the floor. Keep your left buttock grounded. Avoid lifting your left hip off the floor. Hold the posture. Release the big toe. Draw your right knee back to your chest. Straighten your right leg onto the floor. Repeat on the left side.

Asanas.Title: Stage IX
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Deep/Interrupted] -> [LN Out - Deep/Long] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, long exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for fifteen to twenty seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage III
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - deep_curled_tongue] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [Out - deep/ANPC] -> [Slow inhalation with curled tongue exhale through alternating partially closed nostrils, Jallandhara bandha only for exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the chin. Keep the head level and looking forward. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Open the mouth and form the lips into the shape of an O. Push the tongue out beyond the lips. Curl the tongue lengthwise so that its shape resembles a fresh curled leaf about to open. Stretch the curled tongue further out from the mouth. Draw in air through the curled tongue as if drinking through a straw. Fill the lungs completely with the cool air. Withdraw the tongue and close the mouth. Lower the head toward the trunk. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for five to ten seconds. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the tip of the thumb. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and completely through the narrowed right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee once the lungs are empty. Raise the head to a level position. Open the mouth and push the tongue out again in a curled shape. Inhale deeply through the curled tongue to fill the lungs to the brim. Withdraw the tongue and close the mouth. Lower the head to reform the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Perform the root lock (Mula Bandha) and retain the breath for five to ten seconds. Raise the right hand back to the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and completely through the narrowed left nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles of alternating exhalations for five to ten minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling normally through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage III
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Quick In/BNPC] -> [Quick Out/BNPC] -> Quick, short inhalation and exhalation through same nostril 4-8 times then change to other side, Ujjayi then repeat entire cycle 3-4 times
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through the nose. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the tip of the thumb. Inhale and exhale through the right nostril vigorously for four to eight blasts at a stretch. Maintain the same pressure with each blast. Ensure no breath escapes from the left nostril. Finish the blasts on the right side with a forceful out-breath. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Partially open the left nostril with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Breathe vigorously through the left nostril for the same number of blasts as performed on the right. Maintain equal pressure for each blast through the left side. Ensure no breath escapes from the right nostril. Finish the blasts on the left side with a forceful out-breath. Take a few deep breaths as in ujjayi to rest the lungs after completing one cycle of both sides. Repeat these cycles on both sides three or four times. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Normal In - Both Open] -> [Quick Out - Both Partial] -> [Slow inhalation through right nostril and then quick short exhalation through the left 4-8 times then change to repeat, starting on the left side for 4-8 times, Ujjayi then repeat entire cycle 3-4 times]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through the nose. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Half-open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb. Take some deep, steady breaths. Allow the abdomen to expand as you inhale. Inhale slowly through the narrowed right nostril. Quickly close the right nostril with the thumb. Half-open the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Exhale vigorously through the left nostril. Retain the breath for a split second after each exhalation. Repeat this pattern of slow inhalation through the right and vigorous exhalation through the left for four or five blasts in quick succession. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Half-open the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Take some deep, steady breaths. Allow the abdomen to expand as you inhale. Inhale slowly through the narrowed left nostril. Quickly close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Half-open the right nostril with the thumb. Exhale vigorously through the right nostril. Retain the breath for a split second after each exhalation. Repeat this pattern of slow inhalation through the left and vigorous exhalation through the right for four or five blasts in quick succession. Maintain the same rhythm, tone, and volume for all blasts. Complete three to four such full cycles. Take a few slow breaths as in ujjayi to rest the lungs. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (On a bolster)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Place bolster horizontally, sit in Baddha Konasana in front. Take a long belt (or two tied together). Pass it behind the sacrum, over the thighs and shins and under the feet. Make sure the belt goes across the base of the sacrum. Tie it so that the buckle goes between one thigh and calf; pull on the belt to bring the feet close to the trunk. 
Slide forwards off the bolster and hold it firmly.
Lie back over it, with the shoulders on the floor. If necessary, support the head. Tighten the belt, without creating strain. Take the arms over the head.
Stay for 5 to 10 minutes, breathing evenly.
Inhale, come up; untie the belt.

If lower back is stiff or aches, lay the bolster lengthwise under the spine, head supported on a blanket. Use two separate belts, one for each leg, tied around the top thigh and bottom shin. 
Lie with toes against a wall, curling back. Bring the trunk close to the feet. Rest the arms beside the trunk or over the head.

Asanas.Title: Stage Ia
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [BNPC In - deep] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [ON Out - deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (15s) -> [Inhale through partially closed nostrils, Antara Kumbhaka with Mula Bandha 20 seconds then exhale deeply through both open nostrils with Uddiyana Bandha 15 seconds in Bahya Kumbhaka.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Narrow both nasal passages evenly with the thumb and finger tips. Keep the inner walls of the nostrils parallel to the septum. Exhale completely through the narrowly open and controlled nostrils. Inhale slowly and smoothly without disturbing the width of the passages. Keep the septum and fingers stable to prevent the head from tilting. Maintain an even flow of breath in both nostrils. Synchronize the intake of air with the expansion of the chest. Fill the lungs to the brim. Hold the breath for one or two seconds to adjust the fingers. Exhale softly and slowly while maintaining an even rhythm. Synchronize the out-flow of breath with the gradual release of the rib-cage extension. Prevent the chest from collapsing suddenly during the exhalation. Narrow the passages further as practice improves to make the breath flow finer. Complete one cycle of inhalation and exhalation. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the practice with an inhalation. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - ON] -> [Hold (15s)] -> [Deep Out - ANPC] -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils with Antara Kumbhaka then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale deeply through both nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds or for as long as you can without strain. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention with a steady and calm mind. Release the root lock gradually. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the help of the thumb tip. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Inhale deeply with open nostrils until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils. Block both nostrils firmly again to hold the breath. Maintain this internal retention for the same length of time as the previous hold. Perform the root lock (Mula Bandha) during this retention. Release the root lock gradually. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the finger tips. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage VII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Inhale - Deep RN] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Exhale - Deep LN] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, internal retention, long exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage XI
Asanas.Shorthand: [sitting] [RN In - deep] -> [Hold + Mula (20s)] -> [LN Out - deep] -> [] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, long exhalation, external retention]

Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IVa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [BNPC In - deep] -> [ON Out - normal] -> Hold + Uddiyana (15s) -> [Inhale through partially closed nostrils and Antara Kumbhaka with blocked nostrils and Mula Bandha then exhalation through open nostrils with Uddiyana Bandha in Bahya Kumbhaka]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Narrow both nasal passages evenly with the thumb and finger tips. Exhale completely through the narrowly open and controlled nostrils. Inhale slowly and smoothly through both narrowed nostrils without disturbing the width of the passages. Synchronize the intake of air with the expansion of the chest until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils completely by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Readjust the fingers to maintain the narrowed passages. Exhale through both nostrils softly, slowly, and smoothly while maintaining an even rhythm. Empty the lungs completely without allowing the chest to collapse suddenly. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen seconds during this external retention. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the practice with a final inhalation. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified II (Head on Bolster)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Rest the head on a folded blanket or bolster or if really stiff in the head, use a stool.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep/ANPC] ↔ [Out - Deep/ANPC] -> [[Both In] → Hold → [R Out] → Hold → [R In] → Hold → [L Out] → Hold → [L In] → Hold → Repeat → End with [R In] & [Both Out].]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Narrow the right nasal passage with the tip of the thumb. Inhale slowly and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Exhale with both nostrils open softly and steadily until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds or for as long as possible during this external retention. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Raise the right hand to the nostrils again. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Narrow the left nasal passage with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly and delicately through the narrowed left nostril until the lungs are full. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Exhale through both open nostrils slowly and smoothly until the lungs are empty. Perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the abdominal grip and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the last cycle by inhaling through open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.
