Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage V
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] ↔ [Both Out - Pauses] -> [Inhalation and exhalation with pauses while sitting]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale without strain through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Take one long and deep breath at one stretch without any pauses. Fill the lungs to the brim. Immobilize the diaphragm to prepare for the out-breath. Exhale through the nose for two seconds. Grip the diaphragm and hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again for two seconds. Grip the diaphragm and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation until the lungs feel completely emptied. Repeat the pauses four or five times until the air is fully expelled. Release the grip on the diaphragm gradually as the lungs empty. Complete one cycle of deep inhalation and interrupted exhalation. Repeat these cycles for eight to ten minutes. Finish by taking two or three normal breaths through the nose. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both Open In - Normal] ->  [Both Partial Out - Quick] -> [Slow inhalation then quick short exhalation through both open nostrils. Ujjayi after four to eight rounds with Mula Bandha and Uddiyana Bandha if comfortbale]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Take some deep, steady breaths. Allow the abdomen to expand as you inhale. Inhale slowly through both nostrils. Exhale vigorously through the nose. Retain the breath for a split second after each exhalation. Repeat this slow inhalation and vigorous exhalation rhythmically. Perform four to eight such blasts at a stretch to complete one cycle. End the cycle with a final forceful out-breath. Inhale slowly and deeply through both nostrils as in ujjayi. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath within for five to eight seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly and deeply through the nose as in ujjayi. Repeat these cycles of kapalabhati blasts interspersed with ujjayi three or four times. Take one final deep breath through both nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - Curled Tongue] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [Deep Out - BNPC] -> [Slow inhalation with curled tongue exhale through both partially closed nostrils, Jallandhara bandha only for exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the chin. Keep the head level and looking forward. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Open the mouth and form the lips into the shape of an O. Push the tongue out beyond the lips. Curl the tongue lengthwise so that its shape resembles a fresh curled leaf about to open. Stretch the curled tongue further out from the mouth. Draw in air through the curled tongue as if drinking with a straw. Fill the lungs completely. Withdraw the tongue and close the mouth. Lower the head toward the trunk. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for five to ten seconds. Raise the right hand toward the nose. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Partially close both nostrils evenly with the tips of the thumb and fingers. Keep the walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and completely through the narrowed nostrils. Regulate the flow of the out-breath evenly on both sides by adjusting the finger pressure delicately. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee once the lungs feel empty. Repeat these cycles for five to ten minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling normally through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Wide legs)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Initial Position: Lie on your back in a supine position (Savasana). Place your arms alongside your body, palms resting on the ground, and keep your feet together.
Raise Legs: On an inhalation, slowly raise both legs together, keeping them straight (do not bend your knees) until they are at a 90-degree angle to the floor, rest on a wall if needed.
Optional variation to widen the legs while resting on a while for restorative version.
Maintain Position: Keep your back, shoulders, and head on the mat. Push your palms down for support and tighten your abdominal muscles.
Breathing and Duration: Maintain a steady, natural breath while holding the pose for 30 seconds to 1 minute, or for about 6 breaths.
Return: While exhaling, slowly lower your legs back to the ground in a controlled manner, without jerking or lifting your head.

Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Asanas.Title: Stage IIIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep/ON] ↔ [Out - Deep/ANPC] -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the help of the thumb tip. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Remain passive in this empty state without inhaling for five seconds to perform pensive external retention. Inhale deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils again. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril until the lungs are empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the finger tips. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Remain passive without inhalation for five seconds. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through both nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Hold the breath for one or two seconds to prepare for the exhalation. Partially close both nostrils with the tips of the thumb and the fingers. Adjust the fingers to make the inner walls of the nasal passages parallel to and equidistant from the septum. Maintain even pressure on both sides of the nose. Exhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the narrowed nostrils without using any force. Keep the finger tips firm and sensitive to monitor and equalize the volume of out-flow on each side. Empty the lungs completely. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [deep In - LN] ↔ [deep Out - RN] -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale deeply through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose. Place the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Avoid disturbing the position of the nasal septum. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Keep the inner wall of the outer left nostril parallel to the septum. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the left nostril completely with the ring and little fingers. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Maintain a smooth and sibilant sound throughout the exhalation. Manipulate the finger tips to make the nasal apertures narrower as the practice improves to prolong the flow of breath. Repeat these cycles of left nostril inhalation and right nostril exhalation for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IVb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Alt Out - Deep] -> Hold Out (5s) -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating with Bahya Kumbhaka without Uddiyana at the end of each exhalation.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale deeply through both nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face and bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds or for as long as you can without strain. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Release the root lock gradually. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Release the abdominal grip gradually during the exhalation. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Inhale deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils and block both sides firmly. Perform the internal retention with the root lock (Mula Bandha) for the same duration as before. Release the root lock. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril. Release the abdominal grip gradually as the air leaves the lungs. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee once the lungs are empty. Perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) again by drawing the abdomen toward the spine and upward. Hold this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and return the abdomen to its normal state. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [BNPC In - quick] -> [BNPC Out - quick] -> [Quick, short inhalation and exhalation through both partially closed nostrils. Ujjayi after four to eight rounds with Mula Bandha and Uddiyana Bandha if comfortbale]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Partially close both nostrils evenly with the tips of the thumb and the fingers. Take a short and strong breath through the narrowed nostrils. Expel the air with a quick and strong blast through the nose. Repeat this forceful inhalation and exhalation rhythmically through the digitally controlled nostrils. Perform four to eight such blasts at a stretch to complete one cycle. End the cycle with a final forceful out-breath. Inhale slowly and deeply through the narrowed nostrils as in ujjayi. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath within for five to eight seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly and deeply through the nose as in ujjayi. Repeat these cycles of bhastrika blasts five or six times. Take two or three normal breaths through both nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Normal (ON)] -> [Out - Quick (BNPC)] -> [Slow inhalation then quick short exhalation through both partially closed nostrils. Ujjayi after four to eight rounds with Mula Bandha and Uddiyana Bandha if comfortbale]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Partially close both nostrils evenly with the tips of the thumb and the fingers. Take some deep, steady breaths. Allow the abdomen to expand as you inhale. Inhale slowly through the narrowed nostrils. Exhale vigorously through the nose. Retain the breath for a split second after each exhalation. Repeat this slow inhalation and vigorous exhalation rhythmically through the digitally controlled nostrils. Perform four to eight such blasts at a stretch to complete one cycle. End the cycle with a final forceful out-breath. Inhale slowly and deeply through the narrowed nostrils as in ujjayi. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath within for five to eight seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly and deeply through the nose as in ujjayi. Repeat these cycles of kapalabhati blasts five or six times. Take two or three normal breaths through both nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage XV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - LN] -> [Deep Out - RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, long inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation, external retention]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Cross bolsters)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
The Base: Place one bolster horizontally (or a folded blanket) near the top of the mat to support the head and shoulders.
The Spine Support: Place the second bolster vertically (longitudinally) along the mat, with one end touching or slightly overlapping the horizontal bolster to create a cross shape.
Adjustment: Sit on the lower end of the vertical bolster (or just in front of it) and slowly lie back, ensuring your shoulder blades rest on the horizontal support while the upper back is supported by the vertical bolster.
Optional Props: A blanket may be placed on the horizontal bolster to support the neck. If the setup feels too intense, blocks can be used to raise the height of the bolsters.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (On a chair)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Set sideways on a chair with the right hip against the back of the chair. Sit on the whole seat. Stretch the trunk up and take the shoulders back. Line up the trunk with the legs.

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Normal] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Deep breathing, training to lengthen the exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Stage II Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line. Prevent the rib cage from caving in toward the floor. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Place a soft cloth over the eyes to relax the facial muscles. Stay quiet and still for one or two minutes. Exhale quietly through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Avoid pressing down upon the abdominal organs during this initial out-breath. Inhale normally through the nose to fill the lungs. Exhale slowly and deeply through the nose. Maintain a steady and consistent flow of air during the exhalation. Empty the lungs completely without force. Coordinate the release of the breath with the gradual settling of the rib cage. Continue this practice of normal inhalation and deep exhalation for ten minutes. Release all effort and relax the body completely.

Asanas.Title: Stage XV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - deep] ↔ [LN Out - deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Long inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation, external retention.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Quick In/BNPC] ->  [Quick Out/BNPC] ->  [Quick, short inhalation through right nostril and in the same way exhaled through the left 4-8 times then change to repeat, starting on the left side, out through the right for 4-8 times, Ujjayi then repeat entire cycle 3-4 times]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through the nose. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Half-open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb. Take a quick and strong in-breath through the right nostril. Quickly close the right nostril with the thumb. Half-open the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Breathe out quickly and vigorously through the left nostril. Repeat this pattern of inhaling right and exhaling left for four or five blasts in quick succession. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Half-open the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Take a quick and strong in-breath through the left nostril. Quickly close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Half-open the right nostril with the thumb. Breathe out quickly and vigorously through the right nostril. Repeat this pattern of inhaling left and exhaling right for four or five blasts in quick succession. Maintain the same rhythm, tone, and volume for all blasts. Complete three to four such full cycles. Take a few slow breaths as in ujjayi to rest the lungs. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IVa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep/ON] -> [Deep/BNPC] -> Hold Out (5s) -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation then Bahya Kumbhaka without Uddiyana]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face and bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath for ten to twelve seconds or as long as you can without strain. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention with a steady and calm mind. Release the root lock gradually. Partially open both nostrils with the tips of the thumb and the fingers while keeping the inner walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly and carefully through the narrowed nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Release the abdominal grip gradually during the exhalation. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage III
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - normal] -> [Both Out - quick/BNPC] -> Slow inhalation then quick short exhalation through same nostril 4-8 times then change to other side, Ujjayi then repeat entire cycle 3-4 times
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through the nose. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the tip of the thumb. Take some deep, steady breaths. Allow the abdomen to expand as you inhale. Inhale slowly through the narrowed right nostril. Exhale vigorously through the right nostril. Retain the breath for a split second after each exhalation. Repeat this slow inhalation and vigorous exhalation rhythmically for four to eight blasts at a stretch. Maintain the same pressure with each blast. Ensure no breath escapes from the left nostril. Finish the blasts on the right side with a forceful out-breath. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Partially open the left nostril with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Take some deep, steady breaths. Allow the abdomen to expand as you inhale. Inhale slowly through the narrowed left nostril. Exhale vigorously through the left nostril. Retain the breath for a split second after each exhalation. Repeat this slow inhalation and vigorous exhalation rhythmically for the same number of blasts as performed on the right. Maintain equal pressure for each blast through the left side. Ensure no breath escapes from the right nostril. Finish the blasts on the left side with a forceful out-breath. Take a few deep breaths as in ujjayi to rest the lungs after completing one cycle of both sides. Repeat these cycles on both sides three or four times. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep/ANPC] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Out - Deep/ANPC] -> [Both In] → Hold + Mula → [R Out] → [R In] → Hold + Mula → [L Out] → [L In] → Hold + Mula → Repeat To Finish: Final [R In] → [Both Out]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Narrow the right nasal passage with the tip of the thumb. Inhale slowly and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils with the centers of the tips of the thumb and fingers. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Exhale with both nostrils open softly and steadily until the lungs feel empty. Raise the right hand to the nostrils again. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Narrow the left nasal passage with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly and delicately through the narrowed left nostril until the lungs are full. Block both nostrils again with the thumb and fingers. Perform the root lock (Mula Bandha) and hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand to the knee. Exhale through both open nostrils until the lungs are completely empty. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the last cycle by inhaling through open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Bent Legs)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Place a folded blanket on the floor, Square to the room here Neil in front of blanket, with the feet and knees together and inline. Line up the trunk with the legs. Then down, interlock fingers and place the outer elbows four out and hands on the blanket. Keep the elbows directly under the shoulders. Keep the inner and outer edges and hands parallel. Bring the wrist slightly in and cut the hands.
Extend the neck and place the Head down. Be on the centre of the Crown, keep the elbows equidistant from the head. Raise the upper arms and shoulders.
Exhale, bend the legs and jump up. If unable to go up with both legs together, jump with one leg keep the legs bent over the abdomen.
Take the bent legs up and over, with this knees facing the ceiling. Left the shoulders and take the sacrum firmly in.
Straighten my legs up balance here, working on all the points mentioned.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Cross-legs)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Interlock the fingers. Turn the pubs away from you, stretch the arms forward and then up over the head. Do not arch the back at the waist. Tighten the elbows and stretch as much you can.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Normal] -> [Hold (5s)] -> [Both Out - Normal] -> [Includes Antara Kumbhaka]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Take a slow, deep, and steady in-breath through the nose. Listen to the sibilant sound of the breath to control the flow and rhythm. Fill the lungs from the bottom to the top up to the collarbones. Channel the breath to the remotest parts of the lungs. Keep the body and brain receptive to the incoming breath without using force. Draw the entire abdominal area from the pubis to the breastbone toward the spine. Lift the abdominal area up toward the head to massage the internal organs. Keep the diaphragm below the floating ribs to prevent the abdomen from inflating. Lift the inner intercostal muscles at the front of the chest during the inhalation. Create a further fractional lift of these muscles just before starting the exhalation. Maintain the lift of the intercostal muscles and the diaphragm as the air begins to leave the nostrils. Allow the breath to go out slowly, deeply, and steadily. Relax the grip of the trunk gradually as the lungs reach a passive and empty state. Be aware of the fractional pause as the torso muscles adjust between breaths. Repeat these cycles of deep inhalation and deep exhalation for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session with a final inhalation. Lie down in savasana and rest for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage II
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep/RN] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Deep/LN] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left with internal retention, mula bandha, blocking both nostrils]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale deeply through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath after the inhalation is complete. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for fifteen to twenty seconds without allowing any air to escape. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Increase the duration of the retention by five seconds as the capacity improves. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (On ledge)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Place the back of the heel (not the Achilles tendon) on the ledge directly in front of you, stretch the other leg and trunk vertically up. Rest the hands at the sides of the trunk, on the hips or stretch them up.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Cross-legs)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Sit in cross-legs, Lean back and lie down. While lying down, extend the lower back. Take the arms over the head and straighten the elbows. Stretch the whole trunk, especially the abdominal area. 
Stay for 1 to 2 minutes then come up, change the cross of the legs.

Asanas.Title: Stage Ib
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep/ANPC] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [R Out - Deep/ANPC] -> Hold + Uddiyana (15s) -> [R In - Deep/ANPC] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [L Out - Deep/ANPC] -> Hold + Uddiyana (15s) -> [L In - Deep/ANPC] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> Repeat To Finish: Final [R In] -> [Both Out]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without moving the septum. Narrow the right nasal passage as much as possible with the tip of the thumb. Keep the inner wall of the right passage parallel to the septum. Inhale slowly and as delicately as possible through the narrowed right nostril until the lungs are full. Hold the breath for one or two seconds. Lower the right hand to the right knee. Exhale slowly and steadily with both nostrils open until the lungs feel empty. Raise the right hand to the nose again. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Narrow the left nasal passage as much as possible with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly and deeply through the narrowed left nostril until the lungs are full. Hold the breath for one or two seconds. Lower the right hand to the knee. Exhale slowly and softly with both nostrils open. Complete one full cycle by performing these steps for both sides. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling through open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IX
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] ↔ [RN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, long exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for fifteen to twenty seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage V
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Normal] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Training to lengthen the exhalation while sitting]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Balance the firmness of the spine with the mobility of the back muscles. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch just above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Keep the head down as low as possible without strain if the chin does not reach the chest. Rest the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra. Keep the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly as far as possible through the nose. Avoid pressing down upon the abdominal organs during this out-breath. Inhale while ensuring that both lungs fill with an equal volume of air. Expand the chest in an upward and outward direction simultaneously. Synchronize the upward lift of the sternum with the lateral expansion of the ribs. Exhale quietly and release the air. Empty both lungs at an even speed. Adjust the movement of the chest if one side of the lungs moves faster than the other. Maintain this conscious observation of even breathing for ten minutes. Lie down in savasana and relax the body for a few minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Pauses/ON] -> Hold (10s) -> [Out - Deep/ANPC] -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils with Antara Kumbhaka then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through both nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the help of the thumb tip. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Remain passive in this empty state without inhaling for five seconds to perform pensive external retention. Inhale again through both open nostrils using the same interrupted method of short breaths and pauses until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils again. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril until the lungs are empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the finger tips. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Remain passive without inhalation for five seconds. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IVb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [ANPC In - Deep] -> [ON Out - Deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (15s) -> [Inhale through alternating nostrils and Antara Kumbhaka with blocked nostrils and Mula Bandha then exhale deeply through both open nostrils with Uddiyana Bandha 15 seconds in Bahya Kumbhaka]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without disturbing the septum. Narrow the right nostril by bringing its outer portion closer toward the septum. Exhale through the narrowed right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Inhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils completely by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for twenty, twenty-five, or thirty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Block the right nostril with the thumb and keep the left nostril passage narrow. Exhale slowly and steadily through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen seconds during this external retention. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Block the right nostril and inhale slowly through the narrowed left nostril until the lungs are full. Block both nostrils and hold the breath with the root lock (Mula Bandha) for twenty to thirty seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Block the left nostril with the ring and little fingers and narrow the right nostril. Exhale through the right nostril until the lungs are empty. Block both nostrils and perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) for fifteen seconds. Release the abdominal grip and readjust the fingers. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the final cycle with an inhalation through the right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] -> Hold + Mula (20) -> [RN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, internal retention, long exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] ↔ [Both Out - Strong_Deep] -> [Inhalation with pauses sitting]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Inhale for two or three seconds through the nose. Lightly grip the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again for another two or three seconds without letting the diaphragm loose. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation until the lungs are completely full. Repeat the pauses four or five times until full capacity is reached. Ensure no strain is felt in the head or chest throughout the practice. Gently draw the abdominal organs toward the spine. Lift the abdominal organs upward toward the diaphragm. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the nose until the lungs are empty. Release the grip on the abdomen gradually during the out-breath. Complete one cycle of interrupted inhalation and deep exhalation. Repeat these cycles for seven to ten minutes. Finish by breathing normally through the nose two or three times. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage Ia
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In / Both Partial] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Deep Out / Both Partial] -> Hold + Uddiyana (15s) -> [Both In - Partial] → Hold + Mula → [Both Out - Partial] → Hold + Uddiyana → Repeat
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Narrow both nasal passages as much as possible with the tips of the thumb and fingers. Keep the walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Apply even pressure on both sides to maintain equal width in the passages. Inhale slowly and deeply through the narrowly controlled nostrils without force. Adjust the finger tips evenly to equalise the volume of the smooth in-flow of air. Hold the breath for one or two seconds once the lungs are full. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Exhale slowly and smoothly through both open nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling normally through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage XVI
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Inh - Deep / LN] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Exh - Deep / RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (10s) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation, external retention.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage III
Asanas.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Pauses] ↔ [Both Out - Pauses] -> [Inhalation and exhalation with pauses while lying down]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line without caving in the rib cage. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls and direct the gaze inwards. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Place a soft cloth over the eyes to relax the facial muscles. Stay quiet and still for one or two minutes. Exhale quietly through the nose until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Inhale for two or three seconds through the nose. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again for another two or three seconds without letting the diaphragm loose. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation until the lungs are completely full. Hold the breath after the final inhalation for one or two seconds. Exhale through the nose for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for two or three seconds. Exhale again for another two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation until the lungs feel completely emptied. Repeat the pauses four or five times during the out-breath. Release the grip on the diaphragm and the abdomen gradually throughout the exhalation. Complete one cycle of interrupted inhalation and interrupted exhalation. Repeat these cycles for eight to twelve minutes or for as long as no strain is felt. Take a final inhalation to finish the sequence. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep Curled Tongue In] -> Hold + Mula (5s) -> [Deep Out - ON] -> [Slow inhalation with curled tongue exhale through both open nostrils, Jallandhara bandha only for exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lift the inner frame of the chest. Keep the head level and looking forward. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Open the mouth and form the lips into the shape of an O. Push the tongue out beyond the lips. Curl the tongue lengthwise so that its shape resembles a fresh curled leaf about to open. Stretch the curled tongue further out from the mouth. Draw in air through the curled tongue as if drinking with a straw. Fill the lungs completely with the air cooled by the moisture of the tongue. Withdraw the tongue into the mouth. Close the mouth. Lower the head toward the trunk. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Hold the breath for five to ten seconds. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through both nostrils as in ujjayi. Repeat these cycles for five to ten minutes at a stretch. Inhale normally through both nostrils at the end of the final cycle. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IVb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In] → [R Out] → Hold + Uddiyana (15s) → [R In] → [L Out] → Hold + Uddiyana (15s) → [L In] → Repeat To Finish: Final [R In] → [Both Out].
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Narrow the right nasal passage with the tip of the thumb. Inhale slowly and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils with the centers of the tips of the thumb and fingers.
