Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Exhale with both nostrils open softly and steadily until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds or for as long as possible during this external retention. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Raise the right hand to the nostrils again. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Narrow the left nasal passage with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly and delicately through the narrowed left nostril until the lungs are full. Block both nostrils again with the thumb and fingers. Perform the root lock (Mula Bandha) and hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand to the knee. Exhale through both open nostrils until the lungs are completely empty. Perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the abdominal grip and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Repeat these cycles according to your capacity. Finish the last cycle by inhaling through open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage V
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] ↔ [RN Out - Deep] -> [Long inhalation, interrupted exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage Ib
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep/ANPC] -> [Hold + Mula (20s)] -> [Deep/ON] -> [Hold + Uddiyana (15s)] -> [Inhale through alternating nostrils and Antara Kumbhaka with blocked nostrils and Mula Bandha for 20 seconds then exhale deeply through both open nostrils with Uddiyana Bandha 15 seconds in Bahya Kumbhaka.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without disturbing the septum. Narrow the right nostril by bringing its outer portion closer toward the septum. Exhale through the narrowed right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Inhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily without disturbing the width of the passage. Synchronize the fine flow of the in-breath with the expansion of the chest. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb once the lungs are full. Hold the breath for one or two seconds to adjust the fingers. Open the left nostril partially with the ring and little fingers while keeping the inner wall parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly and steadily through the left nostril. Synchronize the out-flow with the gradual release of the rib-cage extension. Hold the breath for one second when the lungs are empty to adjust the fingers. Keep the right nostril blocked and narrow the left nostril passage. Inhale through the left nostril slowly and steadily until the lungs are completely full. Block the left nostril completely with the ring and little fingers. Hold the breath for one or two seconds. Open the right nostril partially with the thumb. Exhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily. Ensure no breath escapes through the left nostril during this process. Hold the breath for one or two seconds when the lungs feel completely empty. Repeat these cycles of alternating nostrils for ten to fifteen minutes. End the final cycle with an inhalation through the right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body and mind.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [ON In - Deep] ↔ [BNPC Out - Deep] -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Partially open both nostrils with the tips of the thumb and the fingers. Make the inner walls of the nasal passages parallel to and equidistant from the septum. Exhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the narrowed nostrils without using any force. Adjust the finger tips evenly to monitor and equalise the volume of out-flow on each side. Empty the lungs completely. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Remain passive in this empty state without inhaling for five seconds to perform pensive external retention (Bahya Kumbhaka). Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Twist Only)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
From Dandasana, sitting on folded blanket(s), bend left knee up and bring foot in front of the pubis. Place inner edge of foot against the right inner thigh. Keep the right leg extended. Turn to the right, and bring the left upper arm in front of the left knee. Place the right hand beside the right hip.
Press the fingertips of the right hand into the floor, and the left arm against the left knee, to turn the trunk more. Rotate the left arm inward so that the palm faces back.
Bend the elbow, take the arm round the lef and then behind the back. Simultaneously, bend the right elbow and catch the left hand. Life the trunk and turn it as far as possible. Turn the head to the right.
Stay for 20 to 30 seconds then repeat on the other side.

Asanas.Title: Stage XII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] -> [RN Out - Deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, long inhalation, interrupted exhalation, external retention]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the left nostril firmly by pressing the ring and little fingers against the nose. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [RN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Normal] ↔ [Both Out - Normal] -> [Normal breathing lying down, just becoming aware of the breath]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Stage I Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line. Prevent the rib cage from caving in toward the floor. Close the eyes. Place a soft cloth over the eyes to relax the facial muscles. Stay quiet and still for one or two minutes. Breathe through the nose at a normal pace. Observe the internal sensation of the breath as it enters and leaves the body. Inhale while ensuring that both lungs fill with an equal volume of air. Expand the chest in an upward and outward direction simultaneously. Synchronize the upward lift of the sternum with the lateral expansion of the ribs. Exhale quietly and release the air. Empty both lungs at an even speed. Adjust the movement of the chest if one side of the lungs moves faster than the other. Maintain this conscious observation of even breathing for ten minutes. Keep the eyes closed and the body motionless throughout the practice.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Sitting on a blanket)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sitting in Dandasana, sit on one or two folded blankets. Follow the normal instructions for Janu Sirsasana

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Sitting on a blanket)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit between the legs, on a height of folded blankets. Stretch the trunk up.

Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep/LN] -> [Out - Deep/RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, internal retention, long exhalation, external retention.]

Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - ON] -> [Hold (15s)] -> [Deep Out - BNPC] -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils, Antara Kumbhaka then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds or as long as you can without strain. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention with a steady and calm mind. Release the root lock gradually. Partially open both nostrils with the tips of the thumb and the fingers while keeping the inner walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the narrowed nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Use the finger tips to monitor and equalize the volume of the subtle flow of air on each side. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage VI
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Training to lengthen the inhalation while sitting]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch just above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Inhale normally through the nose. Exhale slowly, deeply, and steadily until the lungs feel empty again. Attend to the stability of the posture throughout the out-breath. Listen carefully to the aspirate sound of the breath as it leaves the nostrils. Maintain a consistent rhythm and smoothness in the flow of air. Complete each cycle by fully emptying the lungs without collapsing the chest. Repeat these cycles of normal inhalation and deep exhalation for ten minutes. Take a final inhalation to finish the sequence. Lie down in savasana and rest for a few minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Deep] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [LN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Long inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Prolonged inhlation and exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line without caving in the rib cage. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls and direct the gaze inwards. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly through the nose until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Relax the diaphragm and stretch it sideways. Take a slow and deep in-breath through the nose. Maintain a steady and sibilant sound during the inhalation. Ensure that both lungs fill evenly from the bottom to the top. Keep the abdomen from inflating by preventing the diaphragm from moving above the floating ribs. Listen to the sound of the breath attentively to maintain a constant rhythm. Fill the lungs completely until the sound of the inhalation becomes inaudible. Draw the eye-balls consciously downward to gaze into the lungs. Grip the diaphragm at the start of the exhalation. Release the diaphragm gradually as the air leaves the body. Exhale slowly and deeply through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Maintain a steady and consistent flow of air throughout the out-breath. Continue this cycle of deep inhalation and deep exhalation for ten to fifteen minutes. Release all effort and lie flat in savasana to relax the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (On a chair)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Place a bolster in front of a chair and a blanket over the chair's seat and backrest. Sit through the chair, swing your legs over the back, and slide down until your shoulders rest on the bolster and your head is on the floor.

Reach under the seat to grab the back legs of the chair, pulling them to open your chest and stabilize your shoulders. Extend your legs up the backrest or toward the ceiling. For a more restful version, have a partner wedge a second bolster behind your calves.

To exit, place your feet on the seat and slide down toward your head until your back is flat on the floor. Rest briefly before sitting up.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIIIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] ↔ [Both Out - Deep/BNPC] -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds.

Asanas.Title: Stage XI
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Strong Deep] -> Hold + Mula (15) -> [Both Out - Normal/Deep] -> [Antara Kumbhaka with Mula Bandha]

Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Take a strong and deep breath through the nose without any force, jerk, or harshness. Keep the trunk alert and stable throughout the inhalation. Hold the breath after the lungs are fully expanded. Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Raise the side ribs when the body begins to lose its initial grip. Contract the lower trunk starting from the pubis, perineum, and anus. Lift the pelvic floor and the base of the spine up toward the chest to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Lower the head further from the base of the back of the neck to deepen the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Observe the breath as it percolates to the remotest pores of the skin of the torso. Keep the eyes, ears, and tongue passive. Maintain a quiet and still brain. Recharge the energy of the torso by intensifying the upward lift if the throat or facial muscles feel strained. Reduce the duration of the retention immediately if the face feels flushed or the temples become taut. Breathe out normally or deeply through the nose without losing the stability of the trunk, diaphragm, and lungs. Perform ten to twelve such cycles of internal retention. Practice three or four normal breaths between each retention cycle to prevent exhaustion. Complete the practice with a final inhalation. Lie down in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage XIII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Deep/Interrupted] -> [LN Out - Deep/Interrupted] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, interrupted exhalation, external retention]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IV
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In/LN] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Deep Out/RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (10s) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right with internal retention, mula bandha, blocking both nostrils, external retention with Uddiyana bandha]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath after the inhalation is complete. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Increase the duration of retentions gradually as the lung capacity develops. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - ANPC] ↔ [Normal Out - ON] -> [Inhale through alternating nostrils and exhale deeply through both open nostrils]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without disturbing the septum. Narrow the right nostril by bringing its outer portion closer toward the septum. Exhale through the narrowed right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Inhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily without disturbing the width of the passage. Synchronize the fine flow of the in-breath with the expansion of the chest. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb once the lungs are full. Hold the breath for one second to adjust the fingers. Open the left nostril partially with the ring and little fingers while keeping the inner wall parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly and steadily through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for fifteen seconds or for as long as you can. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Block the right nostril with the thumb and narrow the left nostril passage. Inhale through the left nostril slowly and steadily until the lungs are completely full. Readjust the fingers and block the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Open the right nostril partially with the thumb and exhale slowly until the lungs are empty. Block both nostrils and perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) for another fifteen seconds. Release the abdominal grip. Readjust the fingers for inhalation through the right nostril while blocking the left. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the final cycle with an inhalation through the right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Ujjayi proper with prolonged inhlation and exhalation while sitting]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch just above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Relax the diaphragm and stretch it sideways. Take a slow and deep in-breath through the nose. Maintain a steady and sibilant sound during the inhalation. Ensure that both lungs fill evenly from the bottom to the top. Keep the abdomen from inflating by preventing the diaphragm from moving above the floating ribs. Listen to the sound of the breath attentively to maintain a constant rhythm. Fill the lungs completely until the sound of the inhalation becomes inaudible. Draw the eye-balls consciously downward to gaze into the lungs. Immobilize the diaphragm at the very start of the exhalation. Breathe out slowly but not deeply. Allow the out-breath to be slightly longer than a normal breath. Repeat such cycles of deep inhalation and controlled exhalation for ten minutes. Finish with a final inhalation. Lie down in savasana and rest the body for a few minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage III

Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the left nostril completely with the ring and little fingers. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for as long as you can to your capacity without strain. Increase the duration of the external retention gradually by one or two seconds as the practice becomes stable. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Modified II (Near a wall)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Place a folded blanket near a wall. Neil and clasporhands as in regular headstand, with the knuckles touching the wall pure place the crown of the head down, straighten the legs and walking. Exhale, then the legs and jump them up. Rest the feet on the wall.
Strengthen the legs up, keeping the buttocks and here's on the wall. Then bring the coccyx and sacrum forward and stretch up. Learn to balance by taking the feet, one at a time two or three inches away from the wall.
Come down with bent legs.

Asanas.Title: Stage Vb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [Alt Out - Deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (8s) -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils, Antara Kumbhaka and Mula Bandha for 10-15 seconds, then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating then Bahya Kumbhaka with Uddiyana Bandha]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through both nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the nasal septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the help of the thumb tip. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Inhale again through both open nostrils using the same interrupted method of short breaths and pauses until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the finger tips and ensure no breath escapes through the right nostril. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - deep] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [LN Out - deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (10s) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation, external retention.]

Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Increase the duration of retentions gradually as the lung capacity develops. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Deep] ↔ [LN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Stage I Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale deeply through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose. Place the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Avoid disturbing the position of the nasal septum. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Keep the inner wall of the outer right nostril parallel to the septum. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Maintain a smooth and sibilant sound throughout the exhalation. Manipulate the finger tips to make the nasal apertures narrower as the practice improves to prolong the flow of breath. Repeat these cycles of right nostril inhalation and left nostril exhalation for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [Both Out - Pauses] -> Hold + Uddiyana (6s) -> Inhalation and exhalation, both with pauses. Antara Kumbhaka with Mula Bandha and Bahya Kumbhaka with Uddiyana Bandha
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Slowly exhale until the lungs feel empty without straining. Take one long deep breath through the nose without any pause to fill the lungs completely. Hold the breath for two to three seconds. Exhale through the nose for two seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again for two seconds. Grip the diaphragm and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation until the lungs feel completely emptied. Repeat the pauses four or five times until the air is fully expelled. Hold the breath for five or six seconds while the lungs are empty to perform external retention (bahya kumbhaka). Relax the abdominal grip and the diaphragm. Inhale normally through the nose to complete the cycle. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes or as long as no fatigue is felt. Take two or three normal breaths through the nose. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VIb
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - pauses/ON] -> [Out - deep/ANPC] -> Hold Out (5s) -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating with Bahya Kumbhaka without Uddiyana at the end of each exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through both nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds or for as long as you can without strain. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention with a steady and calm mind. Release the root lock gradually. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the help of the thumb tip. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Inhale again through both open nostrils using the interrupted method of short breaths and pauses until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils and block both sides firmly. Perform the internal retention with the root lock (Mula Bandha) for the same duration as before. Release the root lock. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the finger tips. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Pauses] ↔ [Both Out - Strong Deep] -> [Inhalation with pauses lying down]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line without caving in the rib cage. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls and direct the gaze inwards. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Place a soft cloth over the eyes to relax the facial muscles. Stay quiet and still for one or two minutes. Exhale quietly through the nose until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Inhale for two or three seconds through the nose. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again for another two or three seconds without letting the diaphragm loose. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation until the lungs are completely full. Repeat the pauses four or five times until full capacity is reached. Ensure no strain is felt in the head or chest throughout the practice. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the nose until the lungs are empty. Release the grip of the diaphragm gradually during the out-breath. Complete one cycle of interrupted inhalation and deep exhalation. Repeat these cycles for seven to ten minutes. Finish by breathing normally through the nose two or three times. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Head on blanket)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Place folded blanket a short distance from the wall. Kneel in front. Place hands, palms down near the wall. Touch the wall with the thumbs and index fingers, spreading them as far apart as possible.
Raise hips and straighten legs, rest the head on the blanket. Stretch the arms and trunk up strongly. Do not let the weight of the body sink on to the head. Relax the head.
Stay for 30 to 60 seconds then come down.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - BNPC] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Normal Out - ON] -> [Inhale through partially closed nostrils and Antara Kumbhaka with blocked nostrils and Mula Bandha then exhalation through open nostrils]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Narrow both nasal passages evenly with the thumb and finger tips. Keep the inner walls of the nostrils parallel to the septum. Exhale completely through the narrowly open and controlled nostrils. Inhale slowly and smoothly through both narrowed nostrils without disturbing the width of the passages. Keep the septum and fingers stable to prevent the head from tilting. Maintain an even flow of breath while synchronizing the intake with the expansion of the chest. Fill the lungs to the brim. Block both nostrils completely by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose to prevent any escape of breath. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Re-adjust the fingers to partially open both nostrils evenly. Exhale softly and slowly while maintaining an even rhythm. Synchronize the out-flow of breath with the gradual release of the rib-cage expansion. Prevent the chest from collapsing suddenly during the exhalation. Empty the lungs completely. Complete one cycle of inhalation, internal retention, and exhalation. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the final cycle with an inhalation through the narrowed nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VI
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] ↔ [RN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, interrupted exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (On ledge)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Stand facing a ledge 4–5 ft away. Position your feet about one foot apart, then bend forward and place your hands on the ledge. Adjust your distance by stepping closer or further away as necessary to enable the legs to be vertical and the trunk and arms to be extended. Straighten the legs, tighten the kneecaps, and pull up the thigh muscles. Extend the arms and the trunk forward. Take the hips further back and make the upper back concave. Stay here for 20 to 30 seconds, according to your capacity.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [sitting] [Deep In/BNPC] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Deep Out/BNPC]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Narrow both nasal passages as much as possible with the tips of the thumb and fingers. Keep the walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Inhale slowly and deeply through the narrowly controlled nostrils without force. Adjust the finger tips evenly to equalise the volume of the smooth in-flow of air. Block both nostrils completely with the centres of the tips of the thumb and the fingers once the lungs are full. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the right knee. Exhale slowly and smoothly through both open nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the final cycle by inhaling through open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IIIa
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - BNPC] ↔ [Normal Out - ON] -> [Inhale through partially closed nostrils and exhale through both open nostrils]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Narrow both nasal passages evenly with the thumb and finger tips. Exhale completely through the narrowly open and controlled nostrils. Inhale slowly and smoothly through both narrowed nostrils without disturbing the width of the passages. Keep the septum and fingers stable to prevent the head from tilting. Maintain an even flow of breath while synchronizing the intake with the expansion of the chest. Fill the lungs to the brim. Hold the breath for one or two seconds to adjust the fingers. Exhale softly and slowly while maintaining an even rhythm. Synchronize the out-flow of breath with the gradual release of the rib-cage expansion. Empty the lungs completely. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen seconds or for as long as possible during this external retention. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Readjust the fingers on the nostrils. Inhale through both narrowed nostrils to begin the next cycle. Complete one cycle of inhalation, exhalation, and external retention. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the practice with a final inhalation. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage VI
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - deep/RN] ↔ [Out - deep/LN] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, interrupted exhalation.]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Asanas.Title: Modified I (Concave)
Asanas.Full_Technique:
Sit in Dandasana on one or two folded blankets. Lean forward and catch the feet, using a belt if needed. Stretch the trunk up. Make the back concave, lift the chest up and look up. Keep the knees straight.
Stay for 20 to 30 seconds, then release.

Asanas.Title: Modified II (On a chair)
Asanas.Full_Technique: Place a sturdy chair to two and a half feet away from a wall backing onto it. Put a folded blanket over the front edge of the seat one at a time through the back of the chair and bring the hips forward so that the bottom of the second rest on the back edge of the chair. Hold the sides of the chair back. Make the back concave come on exhale, lean that and go down until the back ribs curve over the seat of the chair, just below the shoulder blades. Place the toes three to four inches up the wall, the heels on the floor. Keep the legs slightly bent. Take the arms between the legs of the chair and hold the back legs for sidebars. Straighten the legs by pressing the knees down extend from the point to the shoulders. Take the head back, extending the chin towards appearing against back evenly. Stay for 30 to 60 seconds, breathing evenly, or continue to do the next step. Do not strain the back or the next keeping the legs firm, take the arms over the head. Stretch them down towards the floor. Do not slide off the chair. Stay for 30 to 60 seconds.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep Flat Tongue Hissing] -> Hold + Mula (5s) -> [Out - Deep/Both Open] -> [Slow inhalation with flat tongue, making hissing sound then exhale through both open nostrils, Jallandhara bandha only for exhalation]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lift the inner frame of the chest. Keep the head level and looking forward. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Open the mouth and keep the lips slightly apart. Protrude the tip of the tongue only slightly between the lips while keeping the tongue flat. Draw in air over the surface of the flat tongue with a hissing sound. Fill the lungs completely with the air cooled by the moisture of the tongue. Withdraw the tongue into the mouth. Close the mouth. Lower the head toward the trunk. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Hold the breath for five to ten seconds. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through both nostrils as in ujjayi. Repeat these cycles for five to ten minutes at a stretch. Inhale normally through both nostrils at the end of the final cycle. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage I
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [ON In - quick] -> [ON Out - quick] -> [Quick, short inhalation and exhalation through both open nostrils. Ujjayi after four to eight rounds with Mula Bandha and Uddiyana Bandha if comfortbale]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Take a short and strong breath through both open nostrils. Expel the air with a quick and strong blast through the nose. Repeat this forceful inhalation and exhalation rhythmically. Perform four to eight such blasts at a stretch to complete one cycle. End the cycle with a final forceful out-breath. Inhale slowly and deeply through both nostrils as in ujjayi. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath within for five to eight seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly and deeply through the nose as in ujjayi. Repeat these cycles of bhastrika blasts interspersed with ujjayi three or four times. Take one final deep breath through both nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Asanas.Title: Stage IX
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Normal] ↔ [Both Out - Normal] -> [Observing the breath while sitting]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Inhale slowly and deeply through the nose until the lungs are full. Hold the breath after the inhalation is complete. Keep the trunk firm and alert during this internal retention. Refrain from raising the bridge of the nose or the head while holding the breath. Keep the eyes steady and directed toward the lungs. Feel the breath percolating to the remotest pores of the skin of the torso. Observe the internal grip of the consciousness until it begins to lose its hold. Exhale normally once the awareness begins to fade. Lift the diaphragm toward the lungs to increase the length of the retention as the practice becomes easier. Draw the abdomen in and up toward the spine to support the internal breath. Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds at a time once comfort is established. Monitor the temples and the head for any signs of hardness or tension. Reduce the duration of the retention immediately if any strain is felt in the brain. Transition from the internal retention to the exhalation with a smooth and controlled movement. Breathe out slowly while maintaining the lift of the trunk and diaphragm. Repeat this process for ten to fifteen cycles. Alternate these cycles with normal breathing if any fatigue occurs. Take a few deep breaths to conclude the session. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind completely.

Asanas.Title: Stage XIII
Asanas.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - strong_deep] -> Hold + Mula (15s) -> [Both Out - normal_or_deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Antara Kumbhaka with Mula Bandha, Bahya Kumbhaka with Uddiyana Bandha]
Asanas.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through the nose until the lungs are full. Hold the breath after the inhalation is complete to perform internal retention. Maintain this internal retention for ten seconds. Breathe out slowly and deeply until the lungs are completely empty. Pause the breath immediately after the exhalation is complete to perform external retention. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Inhale deeply through the nose to complete one cycle. Exhale and take two or three deep in and out breaths to recover.
