Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Inh - Deep / LN] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Exh - Deep / RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (10s) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation, external retention.]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage III
Stages.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Pauses] ↔ [Both Out - Pauses] -> [Inhalation and exhalation with pauses while lying down]
Stages.Full_Technique: Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line without caving in the rib cage. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls and direct the gaze inwards. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Place a soft cloth over the eyes to relax the facial muscles. Stay quiet and still for one or two minutes. Exhale quietly through the nose until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Inhale for two or three seconds through the nose. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again for another two or three seconds without letting the diaphragm loose. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation until the lungs are completely full. Hold the breath after the final inhalation for one or two seconds. Exhale through the nose for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for two or three seconds. Exhale again for another two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation until the lungs feel completely emptied. Repeat the pauses four or five times during the out-breath. Release the grip on the diaphragm and the abdomen gradually throughout the exhalation. Complete one cycle of interrupted inhalation and interrupted exhalation. Repeat these cycles for eight to twelve minutes or for as long as no strain is felt. Take a final inhalation to finish the sequence. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Stages.Title: Stage I
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep Curled Tongue In] -> Hold + Mula (5s) -> [Deep Out - ON] -> [Slow inhalation with curled tongue exhale through both open nostrils, Jallandhara bandha only for exhalation]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lift the inner frame of the chest. Keep the head level and looking forward. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Open the mouth and form the lips into the shape of an O. Push the tongue out beyond the lips. Curl the tongue lengthwise so that its shape resembles a fresh curled leaf about to open. Stretch the curled tongue further out from the mouth. Draw in air through the curled tongue as if drinking with a straw. Fill the lungs completely with the air cooled by the moisture of the tongue. Withdraw the tongue into the mouth. Close the mouth. Lower the head toward the trunk. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Hold the breath for five to ten seconds. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through both nostrils as in ujjayi. Repeat these cycles for five to ten minutes at a stretch. Inhale normally through both nostrils at the end of the final cycle. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Stages.Title: Stage IVb
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In] → [R Out] → Hold + Uddiyana (15s) → [R In] → [L Out] → Hold + Uddiyana (15s) → [L In] → Repeat To Finish: Final [R In] → [Both Out].
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is in the lungs through both nostrils. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the septum. Narrow the right nasal passage with the tip of the thumb. Inhale slowly and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils with the centers of the tips of the thumb and fingers. Contract the muscles of the perineum and lift them toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds during this internal retention. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Exhale with both nostrils open softly and steadily until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds or for as long as possible during this external retention. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Raise the right hand to the nostrils again. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Narrow the left nasal passage with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly and delicately through the narrowed left nostril until the lungs are full. Block both nostrils again with the thumb and fingers. Perform the root lock (Mula Bandha) and hold the breath for fifteen to twenty seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Lower the right hand to the knee. Exhale through both open nostrils until the lungs are completely empty. Perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the abdominal grip and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Repeat these cycles according to your capacity. Finish the last cycle by inhaling through open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Stages.Title: Stage V
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] ↔ [RN Out - Deep] -> [Long inhalation, interrupted exhalation]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage Ib
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep/ANPC] -> [Hold + Mula (20s)] -> [Deep/ON] -> [Hold + Uddiyana (15s)] -> [Inhale through alternating nostrils and Antara Kumbhaka with blocked nostrils and Mula Bandha for 20 seconds then exhale deeply through both open nostrils with Uddiyana Bandha 15 seconds in Bahya Kumbhaka.]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without disturbing the septum. Narrow the right nostril by bringing its outer portion closer toward the septum. Exhale through the narrowed right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Inhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily without disturbing the width of the passage. Synchronize the fine flow of the in-breath with the expansion of the chest. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb once the lungs are full. Hold the breath for one or two seconds to adjust the fingers. Open the left nostril partially with the ring and little fingers while keeping the inner wall parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly and steadily through the left nostril. Synchronize the out-flow with the gradual release of the rib-cage extension. Hold the breath for one second when the lungs are empty to adjust the fingers. Keep the right nostril blocked and narrow the left nostril passage. Inhale through the left nostril slowly and steadily until the lungs are completely full. Block the left nostril completely with the ring and little fingers. Hold the breath for one or two seconds. Open the right nostril partially with the thumb. Exhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily. Ensure no breath escapes through the left nostril during this process. Hold the breath for one or two seconds when the lungs feel completely empty. Repeat these cycles of alternating nostrils for ten to fifteen minutes. End the final cycle with an inhalation through the right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body and mind.

Stages.Title: Stage IIIa
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [ON In - Deep] ↔ [BNPC Out - Deep] -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Partially open both nostrils with the tips of the thumb and the fingers. Make the inner walls of the nasal passages parallel to and equidistant from the septum. Exhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the narrowed nostrils without using any force. Adjust the finger tips evenly to monitor and equalise the volume of out-flow on each side. Empty the lungs completely. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Remain passive in this empty state without inhaling for five seconds to perform pensive external retention (Bahya Kumbhaka). Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Modified I (Twist Only)
Stages.Full_Technique:
From Dandasana, sitting on folded blanket(s), bend left knee up and bring foot in front of the pubis. Place inner edge of foot against the right inner thigh. Keep the right leg extended. Turn to the right, and bring the left upper arm in front of the left knee. Place the right hand beside the right hip.
Press the fingertips of the right hand into the floor, and the left arm against the left knee, to turn the trunk more. Rotate the left arm inward so that the palm faces back.
Bend the elbow, take the arm round the lef and then behind the back. Simultaneously, bend the right elbow and catch the left hand. Life the trunk and turn it as far as possible. Turn the head to the right.
Stay for 20 to 30 seconds then repeat on the other side.

Stages.Title: Stage XII
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] -> [RN Out - Deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, long inhalation, interrupted exhalation, external retention]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the left nostril firmly by pressing the ring and little fingers against the nose. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage X
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [LN In - Deep] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [RN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation.]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the right nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage I
Stages.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Normal] ↔ [Both Out - Normal] -> [Normal breathing lying down, just becoming aware of the breath]
Stages.Full_Technique: Stage I Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line. Prevent the rib cage from caving in toward the floor. Close the eyes. Place a soft cloth over the eyes to relax the facial muscles. Stay quiet and still for one or two minutes. Breathe through the nose at a normal pace. Observe the internal sensation of the breath as it enters and leaves the body. Inhale while ensuring that both lungs fill with an equal volume of air. Expand the chest in an upward and outward direction simultaneously. Synchronize the upward lift of the sternum with the lateral expansion of the ribs. Exhale quietly and release the air. Empty both lungs at an even speed. Adjust the movement of the chest if one side of the lungs moves faster than the other. Maintain this conscious observation of even breathing for ten minutes. Keep the eyes closed and the body motionless throughout the practice.

Stages.Title: Modified I (Sitting on a blanket)
Stages.Full_Technique: Sitting in Dandasana, sit on one or two folded blankets. Follow the normal instructions for Janu Sirsasana

Stages.Title: Modified I (Sitting on a blanket)
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit between the legs, on a height of folded blankets. Stretch the trunk up.

Stages.Title: Stage XIV
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [In - Deep/LN] -> [Out - Deep/RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, interrupted inhalation, internal retention, long exhalation, external retention.]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage IIa
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - ON] -> [Hold (15s)] -> [Deep Out - BNPC] -> [Deep inhalation through open nostrils, Antara Kumbhaka then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale slowly and deeply through both open nostrils until the lungs feel completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath for ten to fifteen seconds or as long as you can without strain. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals and lift them vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention with a steady and calm mind. Release the root lock gradually. Partially open both nostrils with the tips of the thumb and the fingers while keeping the inner walls of the nasal passages parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the narrowed nostrils until the lungs feel empty. Use the finger tips to monitor and equalize the volume of the subtle flow of air on each side. Lower the right hand and rest the wrist on the knee. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage VI
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Training to lengthen the inhalation while sitting]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch just above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Inhale normally through the nose. Exhale slowly, deeply, and steadily until the lungs feel empty again. Attend to the stability of the posture throughout the out-breath. Listen carefully to the aspirate sound of the breath as it leaves the nostrils. Maintain a consistent rhythm and smoothness in the flow of air. Complete each cycle by fully emptying the lungs without collapsing the chest. Repeat these cycles of normal inhalation and deep exhalation for ten minutes. Take a final inhalation to finish the sequence. Lie down in savasana and rest for a few minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage VIII
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Deep] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [LN Out - Deep] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Long inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation.]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Lower the right hand to the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage IV
Stages.Shorthand: [Lying] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Prolonged inhlation and exhalation]
Stages.Full_Technique: Spread a blanket folded lengthwise on the floor. Lay another blanket folded three or four times over the first one to support the head and trunk. Lie flat on the back with the spine positioned exactly in the middle of the folded blanket. Keep the body in a straight line without caving in the rib cage. Close the eyes without tensing the eye-balls and direct the gaze inwards. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly through the nose until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Relax the diaphragm and stretch it sideways. Take a slow and deep in-breath through the nose. Maintain a steady and sibilant sound during the inhalation. Ensure that both lungs fill evenly from the bottom to the top. Keep the abdomen from inflating by preventing the diaphragm from moving above the floating ribs. Listen to the sound of the breath attentively to maintain a constant rhythm. Fill the lungs completely until the sound of the inhalation becomes inaudible. Draw the eye-balls consciously downward to gaze into the lungs. Grip the diaphragm at the start of the exhalation. Release the diaphragm gradually as the air leaves the body. Exhale slowly and deeply through the nose until the lungs feel empty. Maintain a steady and consistent flow of air throughout the out-breath. Continue this cycle of deep inhalation and deep exhalation for ten to fifteen minutes. Release all effort and lie flat in savasana to relax the entire body.

Stages.Title: Modified I (On a chair)
Stages.Full_Technique:
Place a bolster in front of a chair and a blanket over the chair's seat and backrest. Sit through the chair, swing your legs over the back, and slide down until your shoulders rest on the bolster and your head is on the floor.

Reach under the seat to grab the back legs of the chair, pulling them to open your chest and stabilize your shoulders. Extend your legs up the backrest or toward the ceiling. For a more restful version, have a partner wedge a second bolster behind your calves.

To exit, place your feet on the seat and slide down toward your head until your back is flat on the floor. Rest briefly before sitting up.

Stages.Title: Stage VIIIa
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] ↔ [Both Out - Deep/BNPC] -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils then deep exhalation with partially closed nostrils to prolong the exhalation]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds.

Stages.Title: Stage XI
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Strong Deep] -> Hold + Mula (15) -> [Both Out - Normal/Deep] -> [Antara Kumbhaka with Mula Bandha]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Take a strong and deep breath through the nose without any force, jerk, or harshness. Keep the trunk alert and stable throughout the inhalation. Hold the breath after the lungs are fully expanded. Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Raise the side ribs when the body begins to lose its initial grip. Contract the lower trunk starting from the pubis, perineum, and anus. Lift the pelvic floor and the base of the spine up toward the chest to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Lower the head further from the base of the back of the neck to deepen the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Observe the breath as it percolates to the remotest pores of the skin of the torso. Keep the eyes, ears, and tongue passive. Maintain a quiet and still brain. Recharge the energy of the torso by intensifying the upward lift if the throat or facial muscles feel strained. Reduce the duration of the retention immediately if the face feels flushed or the temples become taut. Breathe out normally or deeply through the nose without losing the stability of the trunk, diaphragm, and lungs. Perform ten to twelve such cycles of internal retention. Practice three or four normal breaths between each retention cycle to prevent exhaustion. Complete the practice with a final inhalation. Lie down in savasana and rest the entire body.

Stages.Title: Stage XIII
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - Deep/Interrupted] -> [LN Out - Deep/Interrupted] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, interrupted exhalation, external retention]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through the nose until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers. Partially close the right nostril with the right thumb. Inhale through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the flow of air. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through the right nostril for two or three seconds. Pause the breath and hold it for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through the right nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage IV
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In/LN] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [Deep Out/RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (10s) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right with internal retention, mula bandha, blocking both nostrils, external retention with Uddiyana bandha]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Hold the breath after the inhalation is complete. Contract the muscles of the area between the anus and the genitals. Lift these perineal muscles vertically toward the navel to perform the root lock (Mula Bandha). Maintain this internal retention for as long as comfortably possible. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly again by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for eight to ten seconds. Increase the duration of retentions gradually as the lung capacity develops. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage IIIb
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - ANPC] ↔ [Normal Out - ON] -> [Inhale through alternating nostrils and exhale deeply through both open nostrils]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without disturbing the septum. Narrow the right nostril by bringing its outer portion closer toward the septum. Exhale through the narrowed right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Inhale through the right nostril slowly and steadily without disturbing the width of the passage. Synchronize the fine flow of the in-breath with the expansion of the chest. Block the right nostril completely with the thumb once the lungs are full. Hold the breath for one second to adjust the fingers. Open the left nostril partially with the ring and little fingers while keeping the inner wall parallel to the septum. Exhale slowly and steadily through the left nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for fifteen seconds or for as long as you can. Release the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) and allow the organs to return to their normal position. Block the right nostril with the thumb and narrow the left nostril passage. Inhale through the left nostril slowly and steadily until the lungs are completely full. Readjust the fingers and block the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Open the right nostril partially with the thumb and exhale slowly until the lungs are empty. Block both nostrils and perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha) for another fifteen seconds. Release the abdominal grip. Readjust the fingers for inhalation through the right nostril while blocking the left. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. End the final cycle with an inhalation through the right nostril. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body.

Stages.Title: Stage VII
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Deep] ↔ [Both Out - Deep] -> [Ujjayi proper with prolonged inhlation and exhalation while sitting]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch just above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale quietly until the lungs feel empty without pressing down upon the abdominal organs. Relax the diaphragm and stretch it sideways. Take a slow and deep in-breath through the nose. Maintain a steady and sibilant sound during the inhalation. Ensure that both lungs fill evenly from the bottom to the top. Keep the abdomen from inflating by preventing the diaphragm from moving above the floating ribs. Listen to the sound of the breath attentively to maintain a constant rhythm. Fill the lungs completely until the sound of the inhalation becomes inaudible. Draw the eye-balls consciously downward to gaze into the lungs. Immobilize the diaphragm at the very start of the exhalation. Breathe out slowly but not deeply. Allow the out-breath to be slightly longer than a normal breath. Repeat such cycles of deep inhalation and controlled exhalation for ten minutes. Finish with a final inhalation. Lie down in savasana and rest the body for a few minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage III
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - LN] ↔ [Deep Out - RN] -> Hold + Uddiyana (ALAP) -> [Inhale through left nostril, exhale through right, with external retention with Uddiyana bandha]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs until they feel empty. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb. Partially close the left nostril with the ring and little fingers. Inhale slowly, carefully, and deeply through the partially closed left nostril until the lungs are completely full. Block the left nostril completely with the ring and little fingers. Release the pressure of the thumb on the right nostril. Open the right nostril partially while keeping the thumb tip in contact with the skin. Exhale slowly, steadily, and deeply through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and the fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine and lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for as long as you can to your capacity without strain. Increase the duration of the external retention gradually by one or two seconds as the practice becomes stable. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Modified II (Near a wall)
Stages.Full_Technique:
Place a folded blanket near a wall. Neil and clasporhands as in regular headstand, with the knuckles touching the wall pure place the crown of the head down, straighten the legs and walking. Exhale, then the legs and jump them up. Rest the feet on the wall.
Strengthen the legs up, keeping the buttocks and here's on the wall. Then bring the coccyx and sacrum forward and stretch up. Learn to balance by taking the feet, one at a time two or three inches away from the wall.
Come down with bent legs.

Stages.Title: Stage Vb
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Both In - Pauses] -> Hold + Mula (10s) -> [Alt Out - Deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (8s) -> [Deep inhalation with pauses through open nostrils, Antara Kumbhaka and Mula Bandha for 10-15 seconds, then deep exhalation through one partially closed nostril and one partially open, alternating then Bahya Kumbhaka with Uddiyana Bandha]
Stages.Full_Technique: Sit in padmasana, siddhasana, swastikasana, virasana, or any convenient and comfortable meditative position. Keep the back and the spinal column firm. Soften the spinal muscles to allow the torso to adjust during the breath. Lower the head toward the trunk. Lift the inner frame of the chest toward the descending chin. Rest the chin in the notch above the breastbone to form the chin lock (Jalandhara Bandha). Place the back of the wrists on the knees with the arms down. Join the tip of the index finger of each hand with the tip of the thumb to form jnana mudra while keeping the other fingers extended. Close the eyes and keep the eye-balls passive and receptive. Direct the gaze inwards toward the heart. Keep the inner ears alert and receptive. Exhale whatever breath is currently in the lungs through both nostrils until they feel empty. Inhale through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm lightly to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Inhale again through both open nostrils for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted inhalation through both nostrils until the lungs are completely full. Raise the right hand toward the face. Bend the index and middle fingers toward the palm to form mrigi mudra. Place the right thumb on the right side of the nose and the ring and little fingers on the left side of the nose. Block the left nostril completely with the tips of the ring and little fingers without altering the position of the nasal septum. Partially open the right nostril with the tip of the thumb while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and carefully through the partially open right nostril until the lungs feel empty. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the help of the thumb tip. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Inhale again through both open nostrils using the same interrupted method of short breaths and pauses until the lungs are full. Raise the right hand to the nostrils. Block the right nostril completely with the tip of the thumb without altering the position of the septum. Open the left nostril partially with the tips of the ring and little fingers while keeping its inner wall parallel to the septum. Breathe out slowly and completely through the partially open left nostril. Control the smooth out-flow of breath with the finger tips and ensure no breath escapes through the right nostril. Lower the right hand and rest it on the knee. Repeat these cycles for fifteen to twenty minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down flat in savasana and rest the entire body for several minutes.

Stages.Title: Stage XVI
Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [RN In - deep] -> Hold + Mula (20s) -> [LN Out - deep] -> Hold + Uddiyana (10s) -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left, Interrupted inhalation, internal retention, interrupted exhalation, external retention.]

Maintain this internal retention for ten to fifteen seconds. Release the root lock (Mula Bandha) gradually. Release the pressure of the ring and little fingers on the left nostril. Open the left nostril partially while keeping the finger tips in contact with the skin. Exhale through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Immobilize the diaphragm to pause the breath. Hold the breath for two or three seconds. Exhale again through the left nostril for two or three seconds. Pause and hold the breath for another two or three seconds. Continue this process of interrupted exhalation through the left nostril until the lungs feel completely empty. Block both nostrils firmly by pressing the thumb and fingers against the nose. Pause the breath immediately without allowing any inhalation. Draw the entire abdominal area back toward the spine. Lift the muscles up toward the chest to perform the abdominal lock (Uddiyana Bandha). Retain this empty state for five or six seconds. Relax the abdominal muscles and bring the abdomen back to its normal position. Repeat these cycles for ten to fifteen minutes. Finish the session by inhaling through both open nostrils. Lower the right hand to the knee. Raise the head to a neutral position. Lie down in savasana and rest the body and mind for several minutes.

Stages.Shorthand: [Sitting] [Deep In - RN] -> [Hold + Mula (20s)] -> [Deep Out - LN] -> [Hold + Uddiyana (10s)] -> [Inhale through right nostril, exhale through left with internal retention, mula bandha, blocking both nostrils, external retention with Uddiyana bandha]
